params.text:
The fire triangle

Every fire needs three things to start and to keep burning. Together these are known as the fire triangle:

  • Heat – a source of ignition, such as a cigarette, cooker, faulty electrics, a candle or a heater.
  • Fuel – anything that will burn: paper, bedding, furniture, clothing, cooking oil, flammable liquids, or creams soaked into fabric.
  • Oxygen – present in the air all around us.

Remove any one side of the triangle and the fire cannot start or will go out. This is exactly how prevention and firefighting work: good housekeeping removes fuel, safe use of appliances removes unwanted heat, and closing doors and using extinguishers starves a fire of oxygen. Understanding the triangle helps you spot risks before they become fires.

params.taskDescription: Drag the words into the gaps to complete these fire safety facts.
params.textField:
The three parts of the fire triangle are *heat*, fuel and *oxygen*.
Anything that will burn, such as bedding or clothing, is called the *fuel*.
Removing any one side of the triangle will make a fire *stop*.
Good *housekeeping* helps prevent fire by removing sources of fuel.
